title: Selectors, Cardinality, Domain Types, and Extraction Drift · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers selectors, cardinality, domain types, and extraction drift into a changed typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.
Return explicit zero, one, many, invalid, conflicting, and fallback outcomes rather than hiding them behind Find and First.
Parse external strings through constructors for URLs, language, dates, identifiers, enumerations, and bounded text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Selector Scope
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6965 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Selector Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Selector Scope, keep Selector Scope connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Plan the evidence sequence · Lab Selector Scope, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Selector Scope precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Selector Drift
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-selector-drift-1dqyiv" that uses case 4129 to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Selector Drift.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Selector Drift into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Selector Drift, keep Selector Drift connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Selector Drift
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 3013 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: Keep the plausible Selector Drift output and hide the violated typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Selector Drift.

For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Selector Drift for typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Selector Drift condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
